title: Version not bumped for unreleased v1.2.0 feature set
evidence: pyproject.toml:7 & vistab.py:93 = 1.1.3; CHANGELOG [Unreleased] + TODO.md say Colspan Completed in v1.2.0
impact: Release ships as 1.1.3 while containing a feature-level (colspan/CLI subcommands) changeset; users cannot pin the new features by version
recommended_action: Bump version to 1.2.0 and move CHANGELOG [Unreleased] to [1.2.0] as a release prerequisite

title: Extensive LSP/type-hint inaccuracies in src/vistab.py
evidence: ~100+ pyright/LSP errors: draw()->Optional[str] used where str expected, wcwidth param-name mismatch, Optional bool defaults
impact: Runtime-harmless (101 tests pass) but noisy for contributors using type-aware editors
recommended_action: Assess in S2; targeted hint corrections only if low-risk

title: Secrets/PII scan: 290 low-severity heuristic hits all false positives
evidence: scan_secrets.py + gitleaks: 290 low hits all high-entropy-string on README GitHub asset URLs/paths; category secret(275)/pii(15); no High/Blocker; gitleaks (mature) clean
impact: No real secrets/PII — expected for a no-auth/no-network rendering lib
recommended_action: No action; keep .gitleaksignore baseline; consider CI secret-scan (S6)

title: Resource handling clean; stream() memoryless; sort+stream guarded
evidence: All file opens use `with`; only lru_cache is bounded (maxsize=8192); stream() samples N then yields; --sort-by+--stream explicitly errors (vistab.py:3797) rather than silently buffering

title: Colspan width-distribution untested
evidence: _compute_cols_width divmod deficit-spread across covered columns has no test asserting the covered columns widen/sum correctly for a wide spanned value
impact: A future refactor could silently break span width distribution with no failing test
recommended_action: Add a test: wide spanned value distributes width across covered cols and block width>=content

title: Colspan styling parity across a span untested
evidence: set_col_style/set_cell_style/alternating(zebra) applied to a spanned block has no test asserting the span adopts source-column style
impact: Silent styling drift on spans possible under refactor
recommended_action: Add a test asserting span block uses source column style

title: Colspan interactions (max_cols clip / stream / multi-span) untested
evidence: No test for: a span crossing the max_cols boundary (colspan capped); colspan through stream(); multiple spans in one row
recommended_action: Add targeted tests for max_cols-clip and multi-span; stream+span optional
